rdw.source: Elevated RDW is a common finding in RA, reflecting increased red blood cell variation in size.

hgb.source: Anemia is frequently observed in RA due to chronic inflammation and iron deficiency.

hct.source: Anemia is frequently observed in RA due to chronic inflammation and iron deficiency.

wbc.source: Elevated WBC count can indicate inflammation and infection, which may be associated with RA.

plt.source: Thrombocytosis is sometimes seen in RA, potentially due to inflammation and increased platelet production.

mcv.source: Microcytosis (low MCV) can be seen in RA due to iron deficiency anemia.

mch.source: Low MCH is associated with microcytic anemia, often seen in iron deficiency.

mchc.source: Low MCHC is associated with microcytic anemia, often seen in iron deficiency.

rbc.source: Anemia is frequently observed in RA due to chronic inflammation and iron deficiency.
